elements.id: mineru_book_en_国外数学教材-数论-Melvyn B. Nathanson—Elementary Methods in Number Theory_0242_5
elements.text: An ordered factorization of the positive integer n into exactly l factors is an l-tuple (d1, . . . , de) such that n = d1 · · · d. The divisor function d(n) counts the number of ordered factorizations of n into exactly two factors, since each factorization n = dd' is completely determined by the first factor d. For every positive integer l, we define the arithmetic function de(n) as the number of factorizations of n into exactly l factors. Then d1(n) = 1 and d2(n) = d(n) for all n.
